channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: MCAZ tightens border medicine screening
item.description: Tapiwa ChirumeSTAFF WRITER THE Medicines Control Authority of Zimbabwe (MCAZ) will begin screening imported medicines at the country’s ports of entry as... To access this content, please purchase a subscription by clicking the button below and select a package of your choice! Sub #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Dailynews

item.title: ZWG monthly inflation edges up to 0.6% in June as annual rate rises to 4.7%
item.description: THE Zimbabwe Gold (ZWG) monthly inflation rate edged up to 0.6% in June 2026 from 0.5% recorded in May, according to the latest figures released by the Zimbabwe National Statistics Agency (ZimStat). The latest inflation data comes at a time when global supply chains continue to face pressure from geopolitical tensions involving the United States, [...] The post ZWG monthly inflation edges up to 0.6% in June as annual rate rises to 4.7% appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe insists on 2027 lithium concentrates export ban deadline despite extension plea from miners
item.description: Xinhua Zimbabwe will stick to the 2027 deadline for lithium producers to set up beneficiation plants, with those unable to do so encouraged to sign tolling agreements with companies that have the processing capacity, Finance, Economic Development and Investment Promotion Minister Mthuli Ncube has said. The minister’s remarks came after the majority of the country’s [...] The post Zimbabwe insists on 2027 lithium concentrates export ban deadline despite extension plea from miners appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: ZIFA pushing to host AFCON Qualifiers at Chahwanda stadium
item.description: THE Zimbabwe Football Association (ZIFA) is stepping up efforts to have the Warriors play their 2027 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) qualifying home matches at Chahwanda Stadium in Kwekwe, subject to Confederation of African Football (CAF) approval. ZIFA president Nqobile Magwizi revealed the association’s plans after touring the newly-built facility on Friday. Chahwanda Stadium is [...] The post ZIFA pushing to host AFCON Qualifiers at Chahwanda stadium appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: CAB3: Legislators summoned for special sitting
item.description: Nyore Madzianike Senior Reporter President Mnangagwa has summoned Members of Parliament to attend an extraordinary sitting on Tuesday next week to consider and vote on amendments made to Constitutional Amendment... (via Ebusiness Weekly) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Ebusiness Weekly

item.title: Zanu PF pawn Tshabangu pushes to scrap by-elections as Senate passes controversial CAB3 @nehandaradio @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Self-imposed opposition leader in Parliament Sengezo Tshabangu has called for Zimbabwe to abolish by-elections between general elections, arguing that political parties should simply nominate replacements, as the Senate approved Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3). The post Zanu PF pawn Tshabangu pushes to scrap by-elections as Senate passes controversial CAB3 appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Guards disturb Bonda High School teacher’s all-night lula lula with a Form 6 student, refuse his US$2,000 bribe!
item.description: Crisis in the Classroom: Unmasking the Pervasive Threat to Pupils in Zimbabwean Schools MUTARE – A disturbing incident at Bonda High School has once again cast a harsh spotlight on the alarming vulnerability of pupils in Zimbabwean educational institutions. Anesu Mautsa, a 48-year-old teacher at the Anglican-run mission, is currently at large after allegedly being [...] The post Guards disturb Bonda High School teacher’s all-night lula lula with a Form 6 student, refuse his US$2,000 bribe! first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Mnangagwa Calls Special Parliamentary Sitting on Constitutional Amendment Bill
item.description: HARARE – President Emmerson Mnangagwa has summoned Parliament to convene on Tuesday, June 30, a week ahead of its scheduled sitting, to consider and approve amendments made by the Senate to Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3. The Senate has already passed the Constitutional Amendment Bill after introducing several amendments. Before the legislation can be presented [...] The post Mnangagwa Calls Special Parliamentary Sitting on Constitutional Amendment Bill appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Mnangagwa summons extraordinary Parliament sitting to consider Senate changes to CAB3
item.description: PRESIDENT Emmerson Mnangagwa has summoned the National Assembly for an extraordinary sitting on June 30 to consider amendments proposed by the Senate to the Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3. The move follows the Bill’s passage through the Senate on Wednesday, where 75 senators voted in favour of the proposed constitutional changes, four voted against while [...] The post Mnangagwa summons extraordinary Parliament sitting to consider Senate changes to CAB3 appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: ART Holdings Returns to Growth as Revenue Climbs 6% on Stronger Battery and Timber Sales @ZSE_ZW
item.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe Stock Exchange-listed diversified manufacturer ART Holdings posted a solid financial performance for the six months ended March 31, 2026, recording a 6% increase in revenue as improving sales volumes, stronger pricing discipline and operational efficiencies offset challenging trading conditions. The group reported revenue of US$14.3 million, up from US$13.5 million in the [...] The post ART Holdings Returns to Growth as Revenue Climbs 6% on Stronger Battery and Timber Sales appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Mnangagwa Summons Parliament for Special Sitting on CAB 3 @263Chat
item.description: President Emmerson Mnangagwa has summoned the National Assembly to an extraordinary sitting on Tuesday, June 30, to consider the controversial Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment (No. 3) Bill. The special sitting was announced in an Extraordinary Government Gazette published on Friday, June 26, under General Notice [...] The post Mnangagwa Summons Parliament for Special Sitting on CAB 3 appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Citizens Want Jobs Not Amendments, Action Democratic Movement Challenges Zanu PF Regime @ZANUPF_Official

Continue reading "Citizens Want Jobs Not Amendments, Action Democratic Movement Challenges Zanu PF Regime" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Dying for a Russian War: The Tragic Truth About Zimbabweans Fighting on the Frontlines in Ukraine
item.description: Thousands of miles from the bustling markets of Mbare or the quiet suburbs of Bulawayo, a silent and gruesome tragedy is unfolding in the frozen trenches of Eastern Europe. While the global community remains transfixed by the high-stakes geopolitical maneuvering between the Kremlin and the West, young Zimbabwean men are being ground into the mud [...] The post Dying for a Russian War: The Tragic Truth About Zimbabweans Fighting on the Frontlines in Ukraine first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: CAB3 and the myth of electoral peace: Why Zimbabwe’s next parliamentary election could be its most violent @nehandaradio
item.description: In a historical analysis spanning Zimbabwe’s post-independence elections, Dr Phillan Zamchiya contends that CAB3 rests on a false premise, arguing that removing direct presidential elections is unlikely to reduce violence and may instead make future parliamentary contests even more fiercely contested. The post CAB3 and the myth of electoral peace: Why Zimbabwe’s next parliamentary election could be its most violent appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
